Cell.Data: Version SAP S/4HANA 2023 - Standard Scope - 06.10.2025 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

A migration template (Microsoft Excel XML Spreadsheet 2003 file) consists of different sheets which are visible at the bottom of the migration template. You use the different sheets to specify the data that belongs to different data structures. For example the migration template for the migration object 'Product', contains a sheet for basic data, a sheet for plant data, and so on. Some sheets are mandatory, and some are optional.

A migration template is based on the active view of the relevant migration object. You can find information about the active view in the Microsoft Excel XML file. In the file, navigate to File -> Info. You can find the active view name under Properties -> Tags.

In the Microsoft Excel XML Spreadsheet 2003 file, navigate to 'File' -> 'Options' -> 'Advanced'. Under the option 'When calculating this workbook:', ensure that the option 'Set precision as displayed' is selected.

If you are migrating data to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the default size limit for each uploaded XML file is 100MB. If required, you can zip several files together. Note that the combined size of all the XML files you want to add to the zip file must not exceed 160MB. The limit for zip file is still 100MB.
If you are migrating data to SAP S/4HANA, the default size limit for each uploaded XML file is 100MB.

You can increase the size limit for each uploaded XML file to 160MB by changing the system parameter (icm/HTTP/max_request_size_KB). If required, you can zip several files together. Note that the combined size of all the XML files you want to add to the zip file must not exceed 160MB. The limit for the zip file is still 160 MB with the adjusted system parameter.
The 'Field List' sheet is one of the first sheets in the migration template. You use this sheet to get an overview of the expected data in one central location.
It contains information about the mandatory and optional sheets, as well as detailed information for each sheet (for example the expected data type and length for the fields in each sheet).
On the 'Field List' sheet, you can view the following information for each field in the migration template:
The name of the sheet, and whether it is mandatory or optional. Only mandatory sheets have the suffix 'Mandatory', for example 'Basic Data (Mandatory)'. All other sheets are optional.

You can quickly get an overview of the mandatory and optional sheets by looking at the color of the sheet names at the bottom of the migration template. The name of the mandatory sheets have the color orange, while the optional sheets have the color blue.
The group name for the fields in a sheet.
The individual fields in each sheet, as well as whether fields are mandatory for a sheet.
Information about the expected format of the individual fields, for example the data type and length.

In the field list sheet, certain technical information about the fields is hidden by default. The columns 'SAP Structure' and 'SAP Field' (columns 8 and 9) are hidden by default. The column 'SAP Structure' is the technical name of the structure that the field belongs to. The column 'SAP Field' is the technical name of the field. To unhide these columns, select the columns adjacent to either side of the columns that you want to unhide. Right-click your selection, and choose 'Unhide'.

If you want to extract data from SAP ERP, the technical name of structure and the technical name of the field often corresponds to the SAP ERP table name and field name. Also, the SAP Release Note (2568909) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud data migration content uses the technical names of the structures and fields.
For each migration template, you need to specify data for the mandatory sheets, and for the optional sheets that are relevant for your project:
Mandatory sheets represent the minimum set of data you must provide for data migration. Fill in all mandatory fields.
Use optional sheets depending on your migration scope and available legacy data.
Viewing Additional Information for Each Column

In row 8, you can view the field names in SAP S/4HANA, as well as additional information such as the expected format (for example the data type and length). Note that you must expand the row to view this additional information.
Some fields are mandatory, and some are optional. The wildcard character (‘*’) beside the name of a field indicates that the field is mandatory.
Although an optional sheet may contain mandatory columns, if the sheet is not relevant for your project, there is no need to fill the mandatory columns in the sheet with data.

Rows 4, 5, and 6 are hidden by default. Row 4 is the technical name of the structure (corresponds to the sheet name). Row 5 is the technical name of the field (corresponds to row 8 - the field description). Row 6 contains technical information such as the data type and length.
To unhide these rows, select the rows adjacent to either side of the rows that you want to unhide. Right-click your selection, and choose 'Unhide'.
You can view the data type for a field in row 8 (see 'Viewing Additional Information for Each Column' above). Depending on the field, one of the following data types may be required:

Letters, numbers, and special characters are permitted. In the SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit, you can map the values of certain fields with the data type text (usually those fields with Length: 80) to their correct SAP S/4HANA target values. You can do this value mapping in the SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit when you start the transfer (in the step 'Convert Values').

Enter numbers with decimals in the relevant country-specific format, for example 12.34 (United States) or 12,34 (Germany). For fields with decimals, the declared length includes decimals (if required), for example if the information for the column states: Length: 8, Decimals: 3, then a number such as 12345.678 is permitted. Note that decimal places are not mandatory. In this example, you can specify a whole number up to length 8 without decimal places, for example ‘1’. This number would be set to ‘1.000’ internally. For negative numbers, ensure that a minus sign ('-') directly precedes the number, for example '-100'. Note that currencies with more than 3 decimal places are not supported.
Note that the maximum field length supported by Microsoft Excel is 15 digits (including decimals). If you have longer numbers, use the option for transferring data to S/4HANA using staging tables.

Enter the date in your country-specific format, for example 12/31/1998 (United States) or 31.12.1998 (Germany). Note that Microsoft Excel automatically recognizes different date formats and transforms them automatically to the correct XML format.
Enter the time in the format HH:MM:SS, for example 02:52:40

When copying data to a sheet from Microsoft Excel, always right-click the relevant cell and choose the paste option 'Values (V)'. Avoid pasting data that includes formatting and formulas into the migration template, as this will corrupt the structure of the XML migration template.
Using the Find and Replace Function

Do not use the Microsoft Excel function 'Find and Replace'. If you change data by using this function, you may also unintentionally change the field names and corrupt the structure of the XML migration template.
Ensure that you only save the migration template as a Microsoft Excel XML Spreadsheet 2003 file. Other file types are not supported by the SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit.
Do not make any changes to the structure of the migration template, specifically:
Do not delete, rename or change the order of any sheet in the migration template.
Do not change the formatting of any cells.
Do not hide, remove, or change the order of any of the columns in the migration template.
Any changes to the sheets will result in a corrupted XML structure. Such modified migration templates are not supported by the SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit.
Field List for Migration Object: Material BOM
Version SAP S/4HANA 2023 - Standard Scope - 06.10.2025 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.
Base Unit of Measure for BOM(ISO Format)
Unit for Lead-Time Offset for Operation
Ind.: Item Relevant to Plant Maintenance
Unit of Measure for Sizes 1 to 3
Source Data for Migration Object: Material BOM
Version SAP S/4HANA 2023 - Standard Scope - 06.10.2025 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Plant
Key uniquely identifying a plant.
Note: Group BOM if the BOM does not have a reference to a plant. This is a useful option, for example, in the design phase of a BOM which is to be allocated to one or more plants later on
Type: Text Length: 40

BOM Usage*
This key defines the area (such as engineering/design or production) where a BOM can be used.
Type: Text Length: 40

Alternative BOM*
Identifies one BOM in a BOM group. Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field if it's a digital value.
Type: Text Length: 2

Valid-From Date
Specifies the start date for the validity period of an object (such as material, BOM, or routing).
Note: 1. If not set, the system date of the data migration will be used 2. The date format must be the same with the one from user settings, please check the user settings first.
Type: Date

BOM Status*
Key which describes the current processing status of the BOM.
This indicator controls subsequent processing of the BOM in various other organizational areas (for example, engineering, costing, MRP).
Type: Text Length: 80

Change Number
Number used to uniquely identify a change master record.
Note: Please take care when you are using the change number, this number is only used for initial load of BOM creation. It’s not for changes.
Type: Text Length: 80

Empties Unit of Measure (ISO Format)
Type: Text Length: 80

Base Unit of Measure for BOM(ISO Format)
Type: Text Length: 80

BOM Description Long Text
Type: Text Length: unrestricted

BOM Alternative Long Text
Type: Text Length: unrestricted
Source Data for Migration Object: Material BOM
Version SAP S/4HANA 2023 - Standard Scope - 06.10.2025 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Plant
Key uniquely identifying a plant.
Note: Group BOM if the BOM does not have a reference to a plant. This is a useful option, for example, in the design phase of a BOM which is to be allocated to one or more plants later on
Type: Text Length: 40

BOM Usage*
This key defines the area (such as engineering/design or production) where a BOM can be used.
Type: Text Length: 40

Alternative BOM*
Identifies one BOM in a BOM group. Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field if it's a digital value.
Type: Text Length: 2

Item ID*
Identifies a BOM item within a BOM group.
Note: 1.The item ID must be allocated externally only from data migration point of view. 2.The external item id, you must enter at least one character that is not numerical (for example: A0000001).
Type: Text Length: 8

BOM Item Number*
Type: Text Length: 4

Item Category*
Categorization of the items in a BOM according to set criteria, such as whether they refer to an object (for example, material master or document info record) or whether they are kept in stock.
Type: Text Length: 80

Sort String
String which can be defined as required for sorting components for display.
Type: Text Length: 10

Class Type
This is the class type of the class. For the variant configuration for BOM, the following class types are used: 200 = Material (Configurable Objects)
Type: Text Length: 80

Class
Name used to uniquely identify a class within a class type.
Type: Text Length: 80

Resulting Item Category
Categorization of class items according to the object type linked to the class type.
Type: Text Length: 80

Component Quantity*
Quantity of the document specified in Assigned document field, which is entered in relation to the base Unit of measure of the product.
Type: Text Length: 18

Component Unit of Measure(ISO Format)
The system determines the unit as follows: •Material items with a material master record - base unit of measure from the material master record. •Non-stock items without a material master record, document items, and text items - unit defined in customizing functions for BOMs as the default value or modification parameter. •Class items - unit defined in the additional data of the class.
Additional units for material items with a material master record: •For material items, you can maintain alternative units of measure to the base unit of measure in the material master. In bills of material, not all alternative units of measure are taken into account - only the unit of issue. •Possible values are: ◦ base unit of measure ◦ unit of issue ◦ units of measure that have the same dimension as either the base unit of measure or the unit of issue
Type: Text Length: 80

Fixed Quantity
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Operation Scrap
Scrap that is expected in one operation for the quantity of a component to be processed; if you enter a value for operation scrap, you must also set the operation scrap net indicator
Type: Text Length: 6

Component Scrap in Percent
Type: Text Length: 6

Indicator: Net Scrap
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty. This indicator determines whether scrap for the component is calculated on the basis of the net required quantity (the required quantity without assembly scrap from the material master record)
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: PM Assembly
This indicator shows that this item is part of a technical object(assembly) for which maintenance tasks are planned and carried out. (used for BOMs with BOM usage 4 - Plant Maintenance) Note: If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Co-Product
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Recursiveness Allowed
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Alternative Item: Group
Type: Text Length: 2

Alternative Item: Ranking Order
Type: Number Length: 2

Alternative Item: Strategy
Type: Text Length: 80

Alternative Item: Usage Probability
Type: Text Length: 3

Lead-Time Offset for Operation
Type: Number Length: 3

Unit for Lead-Time Offset for Operation
Type: Text Length: 80

Special Procurement
Allows you to define the procurement type more closely. The procurement type is determined from the material type.
Type: Text Length: 80

BOM Item Text (Line 1)
Type: Text Length: 40

BOM Item Text (Line 2)
Type: Text Length: 40

BOM Item Long Text
Type: Text Length: unrestricted

Indicator: Relevancy to Costing
This is an indicator field. Whether you can set or remove this indicator depends on the BOM usage you selected.
Note: Please leave the field empty if it’s not relevant to costing.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Spare Part
This is an indicator field. Whether you can set or remove this indicator depends on the BOM usage you selected
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Item Relevant to Sales
This is an indicator field. Whether you can set or remove this indicator depends on the BOM usage you selected
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Item Relevant to Engineering
This is an indicator field. Whether you can set or remove this indicator depends on the BOM usage you selected. Note: If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Item Relevant to Production
This is an indicator field. Whether you can set or remove this indicator depends on the BOM usage you selected. Note: If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty
Type: Text Length: 1

Ind.: Item Relevant to Plant Maintenance
This is an indicator field. Whether you can set or remove this indicator depends on the BOM usage you selected.
The indicator must be 'X', if the filed 'Material Provision Indicator' is not empty.
Note: If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Material Provision
This key indicates that the item is subject to material provision.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Bulk Material
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Issue Location for Production Order
Type: Text Length: 80

Production Supply Area
Type: Text Length: 80

Price
Amount for non-stock item, which is used when costing the end or intermediate product for pricing purposes
Type: Text Length: 14

Price Unit
Number of units to which the price refers.
Type: Text Length: 6

Cost Element
Number of the G/L account • Which is to be debited with the goods issue, if you have entered a consumption account, or • for which an account assignment check is to be carried out.
Type: Text Length: 80

Account Number of Supplier
Type: Text Length: 80

Number of Variable-Size Items
Type: Text Length: 17

Quantity of Variable-Size Item
Type: Text Length: 17

Unit of Measure for Sizes 1 to 3
Type: Text Length: 80

Required Component
If this class is used as a class item in a configurable object, this setting has the following effect in interactive configuration: the configuration is incomplete until the class item has been replaced by an object. In low-level configuration (MRP, for example), the "Required component" indicator has no further effects.
Type: Text Length: 1

Multiple Selection Allowed
This indicator influences the status of a configuration in cases where a class item has been replaced by more than one object. Only if the configuration scenario allows manual changes to the BOM (order BOM or sales order with manual changes) can a class item be temporarily replaced by more than one object. However, before you save, the selection must be restricted to one object by using a manual object search. If you leave the configuration while the class item is still replaced by more than one object, only the first object is saved or transferred to the calling application. The "Multiple Selection" indicator has the following effect in interactive configuration: the configuration is still consistent if the class item has been replaced by more than one object. Without this indicator, the configuration is inconsistent if the class item has been replaced by more than one object.
Type: Text Length: 1
Source Data for Migration Object: Material BOM
Version SAP S/4HANA 2023 - Standard Scope - 06.10.2025 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Plant
Key uniquely identifying a plant.
Note: Group BOM if the BOM does not have a reference to a plant. This is a useful option, for example, in the design phase of a BOM which is to be allocated to one or more plants later on
Type: Text Length: 40

BOM Usage*
This key defines the area (such as engineering/design or production) where a BOM can be used.
Type: Text Length: 40

Alternative BOM*
Identifies one BOM in a BOM group. Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field if it's a digital value.
Type: Text Length: 2

Item ID*
Identifies a BOM item within a BOM group.
Note: 1.The item ID must be allocated externally only from data migration point of view. 2.The external item id, you must enter at least one character that is not numerical (for example: A0000001).
Type: Text Length: 8

Subitem Number*
Number used to uniquely identify a sub-item within a BOM item and define its position in the sequence of sub-items.
Type: Text Length: 4

Installation Point
Installation point for partial quantity of a BOM item.
Type: Text Length: 20

Sub-Item Quantity
Gives the quantity of the sub-item for an item. The sum of all sub-item quantities is the item quantity.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Subitem Text
Text which describes the sub-item.
The entry field can contain up to 40 characters. You do not have the option of entering a long text as for BOM items.
Type: Text Length: 40
Source Data for Migration Object: Material BOM
Version SAP S/4HANA 2023 - Standard Scope - 06.10.2025 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Material Number*
Key uniquely identifying the material.
Type: Text Length: 80

Plant
Key uniquely identifying a plant.
Note: Group BOM if the BOM does not have a reference to a plant. This is a useful option, for example, in the design phase of a BOM which is to be allocated to one or more plants later on
Type: Text Length: 40

BOM Usage*
This key defines the area (such as engineering/design or production) where a BOM can be used.
Type: Text Length: 40

Alternative BOM*
Identifies one BOM in a BOM group. Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field if it's a digital value.
Type: Text Length: 2

Item ID*
Identifies a BOM item within a BOM group.
Note: 1.The item ID must be allocated externally only from data migration point of view. 2.The external item id, you must enter at least one character that is not numerical (for example: A0000001).
Type: Text Length: 8

Name of Global Dependency
This field is mandatory and used to link the global dependency to the BOM item. For global dependency, the assignment of following dependency types are supported 1) Selection condition 2) Procedure
Type: Text Length: 80

Counter for Sorting Object Allocations
You can use this field to define the processing sequence of procedures, if you allocate multiple procedures to one object.
Values inferred by procedures can overwrite each other. For this reason, the point in time at which a procedure is processed can have a decisive effect on the result.
In this field, enter the number you require to define the processing sequence. The procedure with the lowest counter (for example, 0010) is processed first. The procedure with the highest counter is processed last.
Type: Number Length: 4
Source Data for Migration Object: Material BOM
Version SAP S/4HANA 2023 - Standard Scope - 06.10.2025 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Plant
Key uniquely identifying a plant.
Note: Group BOM if the BOM does not have a reference to a plant. This is a useful option, for example, in the design phase of a BOM which is to be allocated to one or more plants later on
Type: Text Length: 40

BOM Usage*
This key defines the area (such as engineering/design or production) where a BOM can be used.
Type: Text Length: 40

Alternative BOM*
Identifies one BOM in a BOM group. Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field if it's a digital value.
Type: Text Length: 2

Item ID*
Identifies a BOM item within a BOM group.
Note: 1.The item ID must be allocated externally only from data migration point of view. 2.The external item id, you must enter at least one character that is not numerical (for example: A0000001).
Type: Text Length: 8

Name of Local Dependency*
Type: Text Length: 30

Dependency Type
For creating local dependency for a BOM item, assignment of following dependency types are supported 1) Selection condition 2) Procedure
Type: Text Length: 80

Dependency Status
This status indicates the processing status of the dependency. 1 Released 2 In Preparation 3 Locked
Type: Text Length: 80

Counter for Sorting Object Allocations
You can use this field to define the processing sequence of procedures, if you allocate multiple procedures to one object.
Values inferred by procedures can overwrite each other. For this reason, the point in time at which a procedure is processed can have a decisive effect on the result.
In this field, enter the number you require to define the processing sequence. The procedure with the lowest counter (for example, 0010) is processed first. The procedure with the highest counter is processed last.
Type: Number Length: 4
Source Data for Migration Object: Material BOM
Version SAP S/4HANA 2023 - Standard Scope - 06.10.2025 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Plant
Key uniquely identifying a plant.
Note: Group BOM if the BOM does not have a reference to a plant. This is a useful option, for example, in the design phase of a BOM which is to be allocated to one or more plants later on
Type: Text Length: 40

BOM Usage*
This key defines the area (such as engineering/design or production) where a BOM can be used.
Type: Text Length: 40

Alternative BOM*
Identifies one BOM in a BOM group. Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field if it's a digital value.
Type: Text Length: 2

Item ID*
Identifies a BOM item within a BOM group.
Note: 1.The item ID must be allocated externally only from data migration point of view. 2.The external item id, you must enter at least one character that is not numerical (for example: A0000001).
Type: Text Length: 8

Name of Local Dependency*
Note： 1. This field is mandatory if creating local dependencies on an object BOM. 2. Only the dependency type Selection Condition and Procedure are supported so far.
Type: Text Length: 30

Language Key*
Please enter here the language of your description (ISO format).
Type: Text Length: 80

Dependency Description
Description of the dependency.
Type: Text Length: 30
Source Data for Migration Object: Material BOM
Version SAP S/4HANA 2023 - Standard Scope - 06.10.2025 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Plant
Key uniquely identifying a plant.
Note: Group BOM if the BOM does not have a reference to a plant. This is a useful option, for example, in the design phase of a BOM which is to be allocated to one or more plants later on
Type: Text Length: 40

BOM Usage*
This key defines the area (such as engineering/design or production) where a BOM can be used.
Type: Text Length: 40

Alternative BOM*
Identifies one BOM in a BOM group. Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field if it's a digital value.
Type: Text Length: 2

Item ID*
Identifies a BOM item within a BOM group.
Note: 1.The item ID must be allocated externally only from data migration point of view. 2.The external item id, you must enter at least one character that is not numerical (for example: A0000001).
Type: Text Length: 8

Name of Local Dependency*
Type: Text Length: 30

Language Key*
Please enter here the language of your description (ISO format).
Type: Text Length: 80

Text Line
For each object dependency, a documentation can be maintained in each language. The system will separate the text line into several lines automatically in the long texts for each language.
Note: Enter the documentation only if a description is entered in the corresponding language.
Type: Text Length: unrestricted
Source Data for Migration Object: Material BOM
Version SAP S/4HANA 2023 - Standard Scope - 06.10.2025 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Plant
Key uniquely identifying a plant.
Note: Group BOM if the BOM does not have a reference to a plant. This is a useful option, for example, in the design phase of a BOM which is to be allocated to one or more plants later on
Type: Text Length: 40

BOM Usage*
This key defines the area (such as engineering/design or production) where a BOM can be used.
Type: Text Length: 40

Alternative BOM*
Identifies one BOM in a BOM group. Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field if it's a digital value.
Type: Text Length: 2

Item ID*
Identifies a BOM item within a BOM group.
Note: 1.The item ID must be allocated externally only from data migration point of view. 2.The external item id, you must enter at least one character that is not numerical (for example: A0000001).
Type: Text Length: 8

Name of Local Dependency*
Type: Text Length: 30

Source Code of Object Dependency
Maintain the source code for an object dependency here.
Type: Text Length: unrestricted
Source Data for Migration Object: Material BOM
Version SAP S/4HANA 2023 - Standard Scope - 06.10.2025 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Plant
Key uniquely identifying a plant.
Note: Group BOM if the BOM does not have a reference to a plant. This is a useful option, for example, in the design phase of a BOM which is to be allocated to one or more plants later on
Type: Text Length: 40
Source Data for Migration Object: Material BOM
Version SAP S/4HANA 2023 - Standard Scope - 06.10.2025 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Plant
Key uniquely identifying a plant.
Note: Group BOM if the BOM does not have a reference to a plant. This is a useful option, for example, in the design phase of a BOM which is to be allocated to one or more plants later on
Type: Text Length: 40

Alternative BOM*
Identifies one BOM in a BOM group. Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field if it's a digital value.
Type: Text Length: 2
